target.name: Atana Elements
target.founder: Tom Wilson (CEO, Stanford GSB + Royal School of Mines, ex-Hess Corp, was early hire and operations/resource lead at Lilac Solutions from 2019)

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $28M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Atana Elements is a Seed-stage energy and climate company founded by Tom Wilson, whose background spans Stanford GSB, Royal School of Mines, and operational roles at Hess Corp and Lilac Solutions. The company has raised $27.5M to date. The valuation framework draws on a comp universe of three private peers in the energy and climate sector (Hyperscale Power, Voya Energy, Maritime Fusion); no public comparables are available for direct benchmarking.

Given the early stage and limited data availability, stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable approach. Using total capital raised ($27.5M) as a proxy for current valuation and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-Series A progression yields an implied valuation of $69M. This single-method triangulation produces a median estimate of $69M, with a low scenario of $58M (applying conservative assumptions) and a high scenario of $79M (reflecting upside from founder experience and operational execution track record).

The valuation range of $58M–$79M reflects considerable uncertainty inherent to a single methodology. Key sensitivities include the appropriateness of the 2.5x multiplier given energy-sector risk profiles, the representativeness of private peers in an illiquid sector, and the degree to which founder background and prior scale-up exposure justify positioning above the low end of the range. Material value drivers remain pre-revenue execution and technology validation milestones.
